sections.paragraph:
προσβιάζομαι mid. dep.; fut. προσβιάσομαι; 1 aor. ptc. προσβιασάμενος (Aristoph., Pla.) to force to do someth., compel, use force in our lit. only of the conduct of a martyr in the arena toward animals which show no inclination to attack him; abs. προσβιάσομαι I will use force IRo 5:2. προσβιασάμενος by force MPol 3:1.—DELG s.v. βία.

dep. dep. = deponent

fut. fut. = fut.

aor. aor. = aorist

ptc. ptc. = participle

Aristoph Aristoph , V–IV b.c.—List 5

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

someth. someth. = something

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

abs. abs. = absolute

IRo IRo = Ignatius to the Romans—List 1

MPol MPol = Martyrdom of Polycarp; after II a.d.—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 876
